summary:
Overall UK risk remains Elevated. Inflation data show renewed upward pressure from transport costs (motor fuels), keeping the cost-of-living and policy backdrop sensitive to energy/commodity moves.

Operationally, near-term domestic disruption risk remains elevated due to continuing London Underground industrial action (with significant impacts expected across multiple lines). Externally, the Russia–Ukraine war continues to drive elevated European security risk and associated market/energy spillover sensitivity; UK national terrorism threat remains at SUBSTANTIAL.

categories.name: Economic / Financial
categories.rationale: Economic risk is Elevated and slightly worsening on the latest data: CPI inflation rose to 3.3% in the 12 months to March 2026, with transport (motor fuels) a key upward driver, reinforcing sensitivity to energy-price volatility.
drivers: ONS: CPI inflation rose to 3.3% in the 12 months to March 2026, up from 3.0% in February; transport (motor fuels) was the largest upward contribution. https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/inflationandpriceindices/bulletins/consumerpriceinflation/march2026
Trading Economics: Brent crude oil about 103.37 USD/bbl (Apr 23, 2026). https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/brent-crude-oil
OBR (March 2026 EFO): real GDP growth forecast 1.1% in 2026 (central forecast). https://obr.uk/efo/economic-and-fiscal-outlook-march-2026/

Ofgem: energy price cap for 1 Apr–30 Jun 2026 is £1,641/year for a typical dual-fuel household paying by Direct Debit. https://www.ofgem.gov.uk/information-consumers/energy-advice-households/energy-price-cap-explained

categories.name: Military / Security
categories.rationale: European security remains Elevated due to the ongoing Russia–Ukraine war and escalation/spillover risk; UK domestic terrorism risk remains SUBSTANTIAL.
drivers: Reuters: Russia launched major missile and drone strikes targeting Ukraine’s energy infrastructure (reported Feb 22, 2026). https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/russia-hits-ukraine-energy-infrastructure-with-major-missile-drone-strikes-kyiv-2026-02-22/
MI5: UK national terrorism threat level is SUBSTANTIAL (an attack is likely). https://www.mi5.gov.uk/threats-and-advice/terrorism-threat-levels

categories.name: Political / Governance
categories.rationale: No acute UK governance instability signal identified in reviewed official sources; crisis-management activity continues to focus on managing domestic spillovers from external shocks.
drivers: GOV.UK: COBR(M) meeting (23 Mar 2026) focused on domestic economic impacts of the Middle East situation and possible anti-profiteering measures. https://www.gov.uk/government/news/readout-cobrm-iran-economic-and-domestic-impacts-23-march-2026

categories.name: Natural Hazards
categories.rationale: Natural-hazard risk remains Guarded; the England national snapshot shows 1 flood warning and 3 flood alerts (slightly fewer alerts than the prior update), suggesting marginally improving near-term flood readiness.
drivers: Environment Agency (GOV.UK): current national snapshot shows 1 flood warning and 3 flood alerts. https://check-for-flooding.service.gov.uk
Met Office: UK weather warnings hub (check current official warnings). https://weather.metoffice.gov.uk/warnings-and-advice/uk-warnings

categories.name: Social Stability
categories.rationale: Disruption risk remains Elevated due to ongoing London Underground industrial action, with TfL real-time status indicating suspension/no service on key lines during strike periods and broader knock-on delays.
drivers: TfL: real-time status updates during the strike period (e.g., Piccadilly line suspended / no service due to strike action). https://tfl.gov.uk/tube-dlr-overground/status/
BBC: live coverage of Tube strike impacts and negotiations/dispute context (21–22 Apr 2026). https://www.bbc.com/news/live/clyxr6epv7lt

categories.name: Critical Infrastructure / Cyber
categories.rationale: Cyber/CNI risk remains Elevated due to urgent patching requirements for widely deployed perimeter appliances (NetScaler) and ongoing geopolitical-driven cyber risk that can manifest as disruptive activity.
drivers: Citrix: Critical security bulletin for NetScaler ADC/Gateway vulnerabilities CVE-2026-3055 and CVE-2026-4368; customers urged to upgrade to fixed versions. https://support.citrix.com/external/article/CTX696300/netscaler-adc-and-netscaler-gateway-secu.html
NCSC: urges immediate action to mitigate Citrix NetScaler vulnerabilities CVE-2026-3055 and CVE-2026-4368. https://www.ncsc.gov.uk/news/vulnerabilities-affecting-citrix-netscaler-adc-gateway

indicators.name: Brent crude
indicators.note: Trading Economics shows Brent crude oil about 103.37 USD/bbl on Apr 23, 2026. https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/brent-crude-oil

indicators.name: UK CPI inflation (12 months to Mar 2026)
indicators.note: ONS: CPI rose by 3.3% in the 12 months to March 2026, up from 3.0% in February; transport (motor fuels) was the largest upward contribution. https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/inflationandpriceindices/bulletins/consumerpriceinflation/march2026

indicators.name: Ofgem energy price cap (typical dual-fuel, Direct Debit)
indicators.note: Ofgem: price cap for 1 Apr–30 Jun 2026 is £1,641/year for a typical household paying by Direct Debit. https://www.ofgem.gov.uk/information-consumers/energy-advice-households/energy-price-cap-explained

indicators.name: UK terrorism threat level
indicators.note: MI5: UK national terrorism threat level is SUBSTANTIAL (attack is likely). https://www.mi5.gov.uk/threats-and-advice/terrorism-threat-levels

indicators.name: England flood warnings/alerts (national snapshot)
indicators.note: Environment Agency: national snapshot lists 1 flood warning and 3 flood alerts (viewed 23 Apr 2026). https://check-for-flooding.service.gov.uk

changes_since_last_update: Timestamp advanced to 23 April 2026 05:00 UK; overall status held at Elevated.
Economic / Financial trend set to worsening after ONS reported CPI inflation rising to 3.3% in March 2026 (from 3.0% in February), with motor fuels a key upward driver.
Brent crude indicator refreshed to ~103.37 USD/bbl (Trading Economics, 23 Apr 2026).
Environment Agency national snapshot updated: 1 flood warning / 3 flood alerts (down from 1 / 4), supporting an improving Natural Hazards trend.
Social Stability drivers refreshed to include TfL real-time line status updates during the strike period.
priority_watch_items: London transport: monitor impacts through the ongoing Tube strike window and knock-on crowding across other TfL services.
Inflation/energy: watch for further oil-price moves feeding into UK fuel prices and CPI pressures.
Perimeter vulnerability exposure: confirm remediation/upgrade progress for NetScaler ADC/Gateway and monitor for exploitation indicators.
European security: monitor for further Russia–Ukraine escalation affecting energy infrastructure and market risk sentiment.
Flooding: monitor Environment Agency updates where warnings/alerts are active and any rapid escalation driven by rainfall.
source_notes: This update prioritises UK official sources (ONS, Ofgem, MI5, NCSC, Environment Agency, TfL, GOV.UK) with Reuters used for conflict/energy reporting; Trading Economics is used for a Brent price snapshot. https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/brent-crude-oil

EA national flood snapshot extraction did not reliably return the page’s explicit update timestamp in this run; counts are from the live national snapshot view on 23 Apr 2026. https://check-for-flooding.service.gov.uk
